text:
a) The 3-year-old boy needs more milk compared to the 75-year-old grandfather in terms of the requirement per body weight. 

b) The reason for this is that children, especially at the age of 3, are in a stage of rapid growth and development, requiring a higher intake of nutrients, including calcium found in milk, to support bone development and other physiological processes. In contrast, a 75-year-old grandfather's growth and development needs are significantly lower, and his diet focuses more on maintaining health and managing age-related conditions rather than supporting rapid growth.

c) A 28-year-old pregnant mother needs more iron than a man of the same age because pregnancy significantly increases the demand for iron due to several factors. Firstly, the increase in maternal blood volume during pregnancy requires more iron to produce hemoglobin for the additional red blood cells. Secondly, the growing fetus also requires iron for its development, particularly for the production of its own red blood cells. Additionally, iron is necessary for the placenta and for the mother's increased metabolic needs. If the pregnant woman does not consume enough iron, she is at risk of developing iron deficiency anemia, which can have adverse effects on both her health and the health of the fetus. In contrast, a non-pregnant man of the same age does not have these increased demands for iron.
